programme.title: Bloomberg Technology
programme.desc: From San Francisco, Emily Chang presents the only daily news programme focused exclusively on technology, innovation and the future of business

programme.title: Bloomberg Wall Street Week
programme.desc: David Westin speaks with top names in finance about the week's biggest issues on Wall Street

programme.title: Red Lines: US and Big Tech
programme.desc: US tech giants Apple, Amazon, Facebook, Google and Microsoft make up 22% of the S&P 500. Emily Chang explores the calls to break up perceived monopolies and what future regulation might look like

programme.title: Bloomberg Front Row
programme.desc: Nicolas Maduro. Venezuela President Nicolas Maduro speaks exclusively with Bloomberg's Erik Schatzker about relations with the US and Venezuela's economic and political outlook

programme.title: Best of Bloomberg Daybreak: Middle East
programme.desc: A look at the week's most important business and financial issues shaping global markets, with a focus on the Middle East

programme.title: The David Rubenstein Show
programme.desc: Wynton Marsalis. Philanthropist and financier David Rubenstein uncovers inside stories from the most influential minds in business

programme.title: Bloomberg Studio 1.0
programme.desc: Fred Ehrsam. Fred Ehrsam left his position as a Goldman Sachs trader to help set up Coinbase, the largest cryptocurrency exchange in the US. He shares his belief that crypto will change the world

programme.title: Bloomberg Wall Street Week
programme.desc: David Westin speaks with top names in finance about the week's biggest issues on Wall Street

programme.title: Money and Equality
programme.desc: Praatprogramma.

programme.title: A Conversation with Robin Li
programme.desc: Robin Li is one of China's leading internet entrepreneurs with a net worth of about $17 billion. He is the Founder, Chairman and CEO of Baidu, China's most popular search engine. In this interview with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie, Li discusses the Chinese tech landscape and the latest company ventures including its attempts to diversify into AI and pharma, along with his vision for Baidu's future

programme.title: Bloomberg Front Row
programme.desc: Dan Pinto, JPMorgan Chase & Co. Bloomberg talks with Co-President and COO of JPMorgan Chase & Co, Dan Pinto, about his career and his work at the global financial services firm

programme.title: Red Lines: China and Big Tech
programme.desc: Nieuwsuitzending.

programme.title: A Conversation with Robin Li
programme.desc: Robin Li is one of China's leading internet entrepreneurs with a net worth of about $17 billion. He is the Founder, Chairman and CEO of Baidu, China's most popular search engine. In this interview with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie, Li discusses the Chinese tech landscape and the latest company ventures including its attempts to diversify into AI and pharma, along with his vision for Baidu's future

programme.title: Bloomberg Studio 1.0
programme.desc: Jennifer Doudna. Biochemist Jennifer Doudna, co-winner of the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, talks about her work on the co-development of the genome editing breakthrough CRISPR-Cas9

programme.desc: David Westin speaks with top names in finance about the week's biggest issues on Wall Street

programme.desc: Al Gore. Francine Lacqua sits down with the Honorable Al Gore, for the launch of her new monthly series featuring the most respected climate leaders working towards global change

programme.desc: Lisa Su, CEO AMD. Emily Chang sits down with AMD CEO Lisa Su to discuss AMD's impressive comeback story, the company's rising market share and stock price, and the road out of the chip shortage

programme.desc: Brad Stone. We speak to American journalist Brad Stone, author of books about Jeff Bezos, Amazon, and other leading Silicon Valley companies

programme.title: Bloomberg Front Row

programme.title: A Conversation with Robin Li
programme.desc: Robin Li is one of China's leading internet entrepreneurs with a net worth of about $17 billion. He is the Founder, Chairman and CEO of Baidu, China's most popular search engine. In this interview with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie, Li discusses the Chinese tech landscape and the latest company ventures including its attempts to diversify into AI and pharma, along with his vision for Baidu's future

programme.title: Bloomberg Wall Street Week
programme.desc: David Westin speaks with top names in finance about the week's biggest issues on Wall Street

programme.title: Red Lines: China and Big Tech
programme.desc: Nieuwsuitzending.

programme.desc: Robin Li is one of China's leading internet entrepreneurs with a net worth of about $17 billion. He is the Founder, Chairman and CEO of Baidu, China's most popular search engine. In this interview with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie, Li discusses the Chinese tech landscape and the latest company ventures including its attempts to diversify into AI and pharma, along with his vision for Baidu's future

programme.desc: A look at the week's most important business and financial issues shaping global markets, with a focus on the Middle East
